review_title: Simple And Effective Backup
description: SolarWinds saved the day when disaster struck one of our clients, we were able to restore everything quickly and easily as if nothing had happened. Common backup and rescue media is easy to create. Customer support was easy to deal with. We have experienced no issues using SolarWinds Backup and recommend it to all of our clients.
author: Mike M.
pros: SolarWinds Backup is easy and efficient to use. The web interface is easy to use. Just simply install on the devices and you are ready to go. You own your own data. There are no storage limits which is an asset.
cons: There are a few limits on the scheduling features but we have managed to work around them.

review_title: Cove Backup Protection is a game changer
description: Cove has significantly reduced the time we spend on backup-related tasks. Before implementing Cove, a lot of our tech time was eaten up by monitoring backup jobs, troubleshooting failures, and coordinating repairs. Now, those issues are few and far between. The reliability of Cove's backup and restore processes has given us peace of mind and allowed our technicians to focus on more strategic work, rather than chasing down errors or inconsistencies. Cove has helped us operate more efficiently, with less downtime and fewer interruptions. It’s been a game-changer for our workflow, and we highly recommend it to any IT team looking to simplify data protection while boosting reliability.
author: Rick D.
pros: We've been using Cove Data Protection for several months now, and it has truly transformed the way we manage backups. One of the standout features is how incredibly easy it is to use—everything from initial setup to ongoing management is intuitive and streamlined. The user interface is clean and well-organized, making it simple for our team to monitor systems and quickly address any issues.
cons: Honestly, there’s nothing about Cove Data Protection that we don’t like. It has consistently met—and in many cases exceeded—our expectations. The platform is reliable, easy to use, and has streamlined our operations in a way that’s made a real difference. We’ve had a very positive experience across the board.

review_title: Unethical Sales Practices, Difficult to Purchase
description: We had a bad experience trying to purchase this product. In order to get pricing we had to get on a call with a rep. He presented us pricing and asked if we'd like a 30 day trial. We proceeded but in the middle of the trial he emailed pricing that was nearly double what he presented on the call. We had to exchange numerous emails with them before and reach a manager before they finally responded that the pricing presented at the outset will be honored. Then on the last day of the trial the rep sent over yet another different pricing sheet. The product looks okay, but they've made it extremely difficult to purchase. We have a hard time entering into a relationship with an organization that attempts unethical sales practices
author: Dylan H.
pros: Relatively easy, simple product. It doesn't have a lot of control or featuers, but it is simple and easy
cons: Sale rep ethics. The sales rep tried to pull a fast one on us and the buying process has been wastful of our time, disrespectful, and frustrating
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Reliable Cloud Backup for MSPs
description: We've been using Cove across multiple clients for over a 10 years. It's reliable, scalable, and the pricing structure is transparent. It fits perfectly into our MSP offering. The policy-based approach and direct-to-cloud architecture saves time and maintenance overhead.
author: Maxim M.
pros: Cove is lightweight, cloud-first, and very easy to deploy. The web console is intuitive, with centralized control over backup jobs, storage, and reporting. The ability to back up physical and virtual machines, Microsoft 365, and servers without managing local storage is a huge plus. Support response is solid and consistent.
cons: Limited advanced scheduling flexibility. Restore speed for large datasets can be slower compared to on-premise solutions. Would love deeper integration with 3rd-party RMM tools.

review_title: Overall Easy to Use, helps me sleep at night.
description: Good, easy to work with support. Very fast file level restores, so I can look good in front of customers.
author: Athena P.
pros: Very easy to restore, and it verify data is being saved. Fairly easy to manage, and view how all backups are going on the portal. Good support when needed.
cons: Cannot seperate local backups from cloud backups. I need to keep archived backups of data in the cloud, but only have space for so much data locally, and we are very close to running out of room. I do not want to have to delete historic cloud data in order to save current full server backups. Also I wish there was an archive level for old server backups, like the server is not longer in use, but I would like to save one copy of it for the next year, but currently have to use a full license for that.

review_title: Awesome Backup solution for all my clients
description: I love the product and overall have had no major issues or complaints, the backups are fast, and easy to monitor and manage. I have confidence that in any situation I can trust the backups to do their job.
author: David B.
pros: once the Agent is installed, and your data souce is selected, the product just works! With daily reports, I know the status of each and every machine backup. With seeding for large backups, even a slow internet connection at remote sites was not an issue. Restorations are a breeze and have been a life saver more than once when trouble has struck.
cons: during the restore, full paths for restore can be cumbersome with larger data sets, but I do understand their needs

review_title: Best Backup Software Ive Used So Far
description: Overall it's been a very positive experience, very few issues it just seems to work. There have been very few backup failures. So far has saved us loads of time. Honestly it is the best backup software we've used.
author: Andrew O.
pros: Cove supports a wide range of operating systems. The interface is fast and responsive. Backup failures are very rare. Devices are easily to manage individually or via policies.
cons: The disaster recovery options require your own hardware, would have preferred an option to instantly spin up a VM on N-Able hardware, it would allow for VMs to be available almost instantly

review_title: Disaster Recovery
description: Have been using it for over 10 years and have had multiple instances where backups had to be restored to servers. It has worked each time.
author: Leo W.
pros: The Disaster Recovery solution that works. Not very complex to setup. Able to backup a variety of operating systems including server and desktop.
cons: Reporting can be improved. There is no daily email report for Office 365 backups. Pricing should be available online once you log in.

review_title: Excellent solution for cloud backups of workstations, servers, and M365 environments alike!
description: I would say overall our experience has been very positive, and their solution is constantly improving. Their pricing is very reasonable for the solution on offer and they have the best Microsoft 365 backup solution in the business that I've seen.
author: Jacob T.
pros: Their support is phenomenal. Their turnaround time is very good amongst other solutions. Their M365 backup is also quite comprehensive and is generally trouble-free.
cons: Their on-site deployment leaves a bit to be desired -- it's clear they're a cloud-first company. Retrofitting equipment to host virtual machines for the backed-up server images isn't a terribly straightforward process, and though it's been a while since I did so and this is likely to have changed, at the time I tried to do it, it wasn't incredibly well-documented.

review_title: Cove, our most liked backup manager
description: The biggest selling point to Cove is the ease of use, while retention is not infinite, comparing this tool to Veeam, we're happy to have switched. Veeam had us racking up cost both in terms of actual licensing pricing and our own hardware, also the amount of expertise needed with Veeam is leagues above Cove. Cove packs a lot of good features baseline, the modern UI is very intuitive to navigate and it makes restores and onboarding new clients very easy. With good error logging and a up-to-date knowledge database, Cove excels in ease of use, even when things can go wrong.
author: Sebastian V.
pros: Ease of use, the largest selling point. Pricing is very good. Immutable backups are included Feature rich backups with good statistics and intuitive design. Good overview of errors, what went wrong, and often solutions are documented in the knowledge database. Modern and intuitive UI/UX.
cons: Lack of teams 1-1 messaging backup in 365. Recovery verification is fairly expensive, even for servers. Flexible storage locations. Audits only showing user or local user, not which user. Lacking in GDPR & other security statements in terms of searchability, it's easy to say you're committed in one sentence, but customers are usually requiring statements to how data is processed & data storage security within EU/ESS. Lack of exact pricing of used/selected data & amount of clients. We get a bulk invoice and can't digest which customer is responsible for x amount of money in to us.

review_title: My never leave Home without IT.
description: This software is complete in its efforts to do exactly what it is designed to do.
author: Raymond H.
pros: Over a quarter of a century of utilizing Backup programs, cove data protection has provided one of the finest in regards to Backup, recovery, Disaster recovery that I have experienced first hand on the market. Cove not only has performed their due diligence but, provided a greater defense to resolve any missing file issue and to restore sometimes in his little as 30 minutes. Thank you, Cove.
cons: Especially for this review, I wish I could find something that doesn’t feel right about this SaaS and yet I can’t.

review_title: Cove Data Protection - one of the best backup systems on the market.
description: Cove has saved my backside more than once. It's super simple to deploy, restore and manage. from backing up workstations to servers to Office 365(Emails, SharePoint, OneDrive and teams) it's a great all-round cost-effective backup solution.
author: Josh Y.
pros: The ease of use, the ability to see all systems backup success / failure in a single pane of glass. The easy restore process for both Cloud and on prem files
cons: I really only have 2 complaints and I can live with them. 1. The lack of notifications when a backup fails (Although I think this has been addressed in the recent release) 2. The lack of ability to integrate into other RMM's or CRM's for billing purposes.

review_title: Cove is the most reliable tool we have.
description: We've used Cove since the early days of IASO and it's the smartest, most reliable product we have in our portfolio.
author: Simon B.
pros: It's really easy to set up. It's also insanely reliable, it so rarely causes us any issues it's easy to forget it's there until you need it!
cons: The daily reporting needs improvement. The emails are factual but it's difficult to configure them at convenient times.

review_title: Good tool for all of our backup needs.
description: It has been good. We were using multiple tools and processes and we were able to consolidate them into one tool that meets all of our needs.
author: Joe S.
pros: The dashboard provides valuable data and is easy to navigate. The client portals are similarly easy to navigate.
cons: There are a few features that are not intuitive, such as the backup to a secondary device and the restore to another folder.

review_title: Backup for varied configurations made easy
description: Easy setup and quick status checks making backup simple for varied customer configurations. This system is almost set and forget, and troubleshooting issues is simple from just a daily report.
author: Lachlan C.
pros: The integrated dashboard where you can see all your systems and their status at a glance no matter the source is fantastic. And the setup for all sorts of different backup regimes is easy to implement.
cons: Google Workspace has yet to be integrated into the system to make it fully ubiquitious with any Windows based system.

review_title: Cove Data Protection came through
description: An example on where it worked well. One of my clients was using a virtual server. A ransomware attack came from the main server where the virtual server was hosted. We were able to roll back the system state twice during the issue to remove the ransomeware. The client lost no data.
author: James N.
pros: Cove Data Protection was easy to set up, fast to backup and easy to recover systems.
cons: Still unclear on how to easily breakdown the usage per client to bill based on usage.

review_title: Amazing backups!
description: It's been an incredibly great product and a lot cheaper then their competitors.
author: Brandon D.
pros: The amount we save by them not charging by the TB! The automatic restoration testing with email results is also a great feature! Having to buy no hardware is also great!
cons: Sometimes some of the machines will have errors or miss a day of backups.

review_title: Good Cloud backup for MSP
description: Good product for almost every MSP company. When you don't use Kaseya 365 endpoint and User then this is a very good product.
author: ML M.
pros: The interface and the quality off the product , we have used it for several years. and don't wan't to stop.
cons: integration with autotask, the reason we are switching to Datto Backup is the because off the integrations with Autotask and Datto RMM.

review_title: better than storagecraft
description: Been using it for years, when arcserve bought storagecraft and then deleted all the backups it was time to move and this is where I moved to. Better than storagecraft, use macrium to fix boot problems that sometime arise after restore.
author: Iain F.
pros: it works, quick and fast cloud backup. Its simple to install and maintain. it backups and restores quickly and painlessly
cons: support. Support is lacking if you are not familiar with product you will need support, that's when things become unstuck. The documentation is comprehensive but is keyword based, wrong keyword and you are not finding it, think bsod rather blue screen.

review_title: Cove Data Protection is our IT shield
description: I work in the medical industry, an as an IT security consultant, I value keeping all of our patient data safe. Cove Data helps me to: 1) Automatically create backups, where I schedule to save sensitive patient data, such as records and consents. 2) I conduct mock security breaches and data recovery drills. This ensures that I have action plans in case the clinic suffers a real attack and, of course, that I can make immediate recoveries to prevent the clinic's operations from grinding to a halt. 3) I always have real-time alerts active in case the system fails. I haven't had any problems with the software, but the alerts would warn me if there is any issue.
author: Nour N.
pros: Backups are immutable and isolated from the local network, so ransomware risks are almost unlikely. This feature was a must for me before implementing security software. The backups are small because they are not handled as files but at the bit level. I can make backups constantly without saturating the network. All backup management is easy and accessible from a single panel. If I need something, it will be available very quickly, even during disasters. Of course, the most important thing here is that there are many recovery options, so one of them will always be available.
cons: Small data recovery is fast, but restoring large volumes can take time. This is a preoccupation for us when we have images with prescriptions and signed consent documents. It's not a problem right now because we haven't had vulnerabilities. Still, if we needed to do a full recovery, it would take time to get the clinic backup and running.

review_title: It just works
description: It works! We have used it to restore data and critical systems. We use this to test our disaster recovery process/policy as well.
author: Michael G.
pros: Great tool for file level and system restores. Physical to Physical, VM to VM, Physical to VM, it works effortlessly and efficiently. Cloud immutable backups are key! Simple to use reporting and alerting for backupsuccess/fail.
cons: Licensing model is pretty bad. Still have to pay for a server license that is no longer in use! Should be able to scale as needed without effort with monthly billing, but I have been told that is not possible. Just sucks that you have to pay a years worth of licensing that simply will never be used becuase a server crashed and you moved the data to another already licensed.

review_title: Simplicity and customizible
description: I haven't had much problem with the product, it is very solid and really delivers a lot of customization.
author: Bobo L.
pros: Quick and easy to set up, a lot of possibilities to custom different packages/offers. Love the white label option so I can brand it myself.
cons: As a backup service there isn't much to dislike about it, fairly complete product for my needs as a smaller MSP.

review_title: Simple, quick and reliable!
description: Cove Data Protection has made my job easier and taken the burden of worrying about backups off my plate. We have had to use it several times to restore files that my customers have lost or just needed an old copy of and it has not let us down so far.
author: Rick B.
pros: Cove Data Protection is easy to setup and configure. It has plenty of options and is very reliable. File restoration is quick, simple and straightforward.
cons: Honestly I have no complaints at this time. If anything, the interface could use an update but it is functional the way it is.

review_title: Cove Data Protection can save the day
description: Cove Data Protection has been fantastic and I recommend it to others. It has saved the day several times.
author: Dan C.
pros: We've had good luck with Cove Data Protection. We've been using it for several years and have never had an issue. It has saved the day several times.
cons: The only con I would have is that data overages can be unexpected. As an MSP, our customers can sometimes surprise us with more data. This is not necessarily a Cove issue.

review_title: Review of Cove Data Protection
description: I have customers with data bases that need to be protected and when customers loss the data. Cove Data Protection save the day Support is very professional If there is a special problem to be solved Your case gets transferred to the Head Nerd which is rare because their technicians are Very Good, but no one knows everything The problem has always been solved.
author: Joe S.
pros: You Get support when you need it 24 hours a day, In the United States and in England and the Philippines
cons: price commitment yearly contract. I do not like to be locked into a commitment Would rather have paid as you go term.
